title: Little Lighthouse charity tee: rebuild two raster concept mockups into clean 3-spot-color screen-print separations (front chest + full back) as print-ready vector

source.reference: Vector Apparel Designer Needed to Create Screen Print Ready T-Shirt Artwork

grounding_note: VERBATIM from the Upwork brief: "recreate and refine several existing t-shirt concepts into professional, production-ready vector artwork for screen printing"; "This project is for a children's charity fundraising campaign and includes both front chest and full-back shirt designs"; "We already have concept artwork and direction established"; "Recreate existing shirt concepts as clean vector artwork"; "Create front chest and full-back designs"; "Prepare artwork for screen printing"; "Follow provided brand guidelines and color standards"; "Convert all fonts to outlines"; "No gradients, halftones". REASONABLY ENRICHED to make it a runnable Adobe-only benchmark: the real brief is a rebuild-and-prep job (take supplied flat raster concepts + a fixed brand color list and turn them into clean production-ready vector separations) — this is an EDIT/transform of supplied assets, NOT from-scratch generative art, so it stays fully in connector scope. The client is modeled as supplying two flat, high-contrast raster concept mockups (front chest + full back), a brand-guidelines PDF pinning the exact spot colors, and the printer's mesh/max-color constraint (max 3 spot colors, no gradients, no halftones, fonts already flattened into the raster). The Adobe chain does the genuinely-doable heavy lifting the brief rewards: isolate each brand color region via prompt selection, FORCE it to the exact approved PANTONE hex as a SOLID flat fill (image_fill_area solid-fill), drop the muddy paper-texture background to transparent, vectorize each cleaned single-color layer into crisp scalable vector, and export per-color separation plates plus a combined print-ready proof PDF. No color the printer didn't approve is introduced; nothing is composed from scratch — supplied art and its already-flattened lettering are only transformed.
one_line_ask: Rebuild the client's two flat raster tee concepts (front chest + full back) into clean, production-ready screen-print separations: isolate each of the 3 approved brand spot colors and force it to its exact PANTONE hex as a solid flat plate, vectorize every color layer, and export per-color separation vectors plus a combined proof PDF.
full_brief: Little Lighthouse Fund, a children's charity, is running a fundraising T-shirt campaign and needs its two established concepts turned into production-ready screen-print artwork. The design lead has handed off two flat, high-contrast raster concept mockups exported from their design tool — a front-chest lockup (a stylized lighthouse-and-wave badge with the words LITTLE LIGHTHOUSE) and a full-back design (a larger lighthouse scene with a supporter tagline) — plus a one-page brand-guidelines PDF that pins the campaign palette to exactly three approved spot colors: Beacon Red (PANTONE 186 C, hex #C8102E), Harbor Navy (PANTONE 2758 C, hex #1B2A5B), and Sand Cream (PANTONE 7401 C, hex #F0E6C8). The printer's constraints are strict and non-negotiable: MAXIMUM 3 spot colors per shirt, NO gradients, NO halftones, all lettering already flattened into the raster (no live fonts needed), and each color must sit on its own clean, solid separation so it can be burned to a screen. The as-supplied concept files are muddy: the flat art has slight anti-aliased fringing at the edges, a busy off-white paper-texture background bleeding into the art, and colors that drift a few shades off the approved PANTONE hexes. The job: (1) for BOTH the front and back art, isolate each of the three brand regions by prompt selection and force it to the exact approved spot-color hex as a solid flat fill (no gradient, no tint ramp) so each plate is press-clean; (2) drop the paper-texture background to transparent so only the artwork remains; (3) vectorize each cleaned, single-color layer into crisp scalable vector so the plates hold a hard edge at full front/back print size; (4) deliver one separation vector per color per placement (6 plates total: front R/N/C + back R/N/C), a transparent-background composite vector of each full design, and a single combined proof PDF the printer can check color count and registration against; (5) stage everything into a tidy CC folder and drop a print-shop recipe note listing the PANTONE numbers, placement sizes, and mesh guidance so the printer can run it without a call. Front chest prints ~10cm wide, full back ~30cm wide. Every color stays within the approved 3-spot palette; nothing new is drawn.

inputs.name: concept_front_chest.png
inputs.gen_prompt: A flat, high-contrast T-shirt front-chest graphic concept mockup: a stylized circular badge of a lighthouse over stylized wave lines with a beam, and bold flattened lettering reading 'LITTLE LIGHTHOUSE' arced around it. Rendered in three loosely-separated colors — a warm red, a dark navy, and a cream — but the reds and navies drift a few shades off-target and edges are slightly anti-aliased/fuzzy. Sitting on a busy off-white paper-texture background that bleeds faintly into the art. Flat raster PNG export, no gradients intended but some soft fringing present, looks like a real design-tool concept export not a clean vector.
inputs.realism_notes: Deliberately imperfect (off-target hues, anti-aliased fringing, paper-texture bg) so the isolate->force-to-spot-color->vectorize chain has real work; flattened lettering so no live-font/text-to-image step is needed. gemini-3-pro-image for clean legible lettering.

inputs.name: concept_full_back.png
inputs.gen_prompt: A flat, high-contrast T-shirt full-back graphic concept mockup: a larger lighthouse scene on a rocky point with stylized wave bands and a beam, and a flattened supporter tagline 'SHINE A LIGHT FOR EVERY KID' below it. Same three loosely-separated colors as the front — warm red, dark navy, cream — with hues drifting a few shades off and slightly fuzzy anti-aliased edges, on a busy off-white paper-texture background. Flat raster PNG, looks like a real concept export, not clean vector.
inputs.realism_notes: Second placement; same imperfections and same 3-color palette so both can be separated to a consistent approved spot-color set. Larger art justifies the same isolate/fill/vectorize pipeline run again.

inputs.name: brand_guidelines_palette.pdf
inputs.gen_prompt: Generate a concise one-page brand-guidelines PDF for the fictional 'Little Lighthouse Fund' children's charity apparel campaign. Must pin the palette to exactly three approved spot colors with PANTONE numbers and hex: Beacon Red (PANTONE 186 C, #C8102E), Harbor Navy (PANTONE 2758 C, #1B2A5B), Sand Cream (PANTONE 7401 C, #F0E6C8). State the printer rules: max 3 spot colors, no gradients, no halftones, each color on its own separation. Include front-chest print size ~10cm wide and full-back print size ~30cm wide.
inputs.realism_notes: Supplies the fixed, printer-approved color list + rules that make this an EDIT-to-spec job (force supplied art to these exact hexes) rather than a creative/generative one. Drives the exact fill hexes and the recipe note.

inputs.name: printer_specsheet.txt
inputs.gen_prompt: Generate a short plain-text screen printer spec sheet for a charity tee run: 'Max 3 spot colors. Deliver each color as its own solid separation (vector preferred). No gradients, no halftones, no 4-color process. Fonts must be outlined/flattened. Front chest max 10cm wide, full back max 30cm wide. Provide a combined color proof PDF for registration check. Mesh: 156 for line art. Underbase not required on light garments.'
inputs.realism_notes: The 'make revisions based on feedback from our printer' element of the brief; its constraints (per-color solid seps, no gradient/halftone, outlined fonts) are exactly what the connector chain honors and what the recipe note echoes back.

outputs.name: front_sep_red_vector.svg
outputs.spec: Front-chest Beacon Red (PANTONE 186 C, #C8102E) separation plate as a single-color vector SVG on transparent background, sized for ~10cm print width. Solid flat fill only — no gradient, no halftone — ready to burn to its own screen.

outputs.name: front_sep_navy_vector.svg
outputs.spec: Front-chest Harbor Navy (PANTONE 2758 C, #1B2A5B) separation plate as a single-color vector SVG on transparent background, sized for ~10cm print width. Solid flat fill only, its own screen.

outputs.name: front_sep_cream_vector.svg
outputs.spec: Front-chest Sand Cream (PANTONE 7401 C, #F0E6C8) separation plate as a single-color vector SVG on transparent background, sized for ~10cm print width, derived as NOT(red+navy). Solid flat fill only, its own screen.

outputs.name: back_sep_red_vector.svg
outputs.spec: Full-back Beacon Red (PANTONE 186 C, #C8102E) separation plate as a single-color vector SVG on transparent background, sized for ~30cm print width. Solid flat fill only, its own screen.

outputs.name: back_sep_navy_vector.svg
outputs.spec: Full-back Harbor Navy (PANTONE 2758 C, #1B2A5B) separation plate as a single-color vector SVG on transparent background, sized for ~30cm print width. Solid flat fill only, its own screen.

outputs.name: back_sep_cream_vector.svg
outputs.spec: Full-back Sand Cream (PANTONE 7401 C, #F0E6C8) separation plate as a single-color vector SVG on transparent background, sized for ~30cm print width, derived as NOT(red+navy). Solid flat fill only, its own screen.

outputs.name: front_composite.svg
outputs.spec: Transparent-background composite vector SVG of the full cleaned three-color front-chest design (all three approved spot colors in registration), for client approval/placement reference at ~10cm print width.

outputs.name: back_composite.svg
outputs.spec: Transparent-background composite vector SVG of the full cleaned three-color full-back design (all three approved spot colors in registration), for client approval/placement reference at ~30cm print width.

outputs.name: Little_Lighthouse_seps_proof.pdf
outputs.spec: Two-page print-ready proof PDF (front composite on page 1, back composite on page 2) rendered from the two full-design composite vectors, each page showing all three approved spot colors in registration so the printer can verify the 3-color count and alignment before burning screens. Per-plate PANTONE labels and separation order are provided alongside in the recipe note rather than baked into this vector render.

outputs.name: printshop_recipe_note.txt
outputs.spec: Plain-text print-shop hand-off note listing the three approved PANTONE numbers + hexes (Beacon Red 186 C #C8102E, Harbor Navy 2758 C #1B2A5B, Sand Cream 7401 C #F0E6C8), per-color separation order mapping each uploaded plate URN to its PANTONE, front ~10cm / back ~30cm placement sizes, mesh 156 for line art, and 'no underbase on light garment' guidance so the shop can run the job without a call.

connector_workflow.note: Create the tidy CC project folder structure (separations + composites + proof + hand-off) so every plate produced downstream has a home for the printer hand-off.
Begin the front placement: select the red region of the front-chest art to isolate the Beacon Red plate.
Solid-fill the front red region with exact Beacon Red #C8102E to force it to the approved spot color (flat fill, no gradient).
Select the navy region of the front art for its Harbor Navy plate.
Solid-fill the front navy region with exact Harbor Navy #1B2A5B.
Select the cream/sand region of the front art to isolate its third plate.
Solid-fill the front cream region with exact Sand Cream #F0E6C8 so all three front colors are forced to their approved flat spot hexes.

Knock down the saturation of the off-white paper-texture band only (not the three brand colors), making the background a neutral near-white so it lifts cleanly to transparent in the next step. No brand-art recolor.
Drop the paper-texture background on the front art to transparent so only the three-color artwork remains.
Isolate the Beacon Red areas on the cleaned front art for its separation plate.
Vectorize the front Beacon Red layer into a solid vector plate sized for ~10cm print width.
Isolate the Harbor Navy areas of the cleaned front art.
Vectorize the front Harbor Navy layer into a solid vector separation.

Select the two SATURATED brand colors together — 'all the bright-red and dark-navy artwork marks' — a robust high-contrast prompt, rather than trying to pick the near-white Sand Cream directly off a transparent background.

Invert the red+navy selection so the remaining opaque artwork pixels — the Sand Cream fill — become the third mask. Deriving cream geometrically as NOT(red+navy) avoids the shaky near-white-on-transparency selection entirely.
Vectorize the flat Sand Cream layer (the inverted-mask region) into the third solid front separation plate.
Vectorize the full cleaned three-color front art into one transparent-background composite SVG for client approval/placement reference.
Begin the back placement: select its red region to isolate the Beacon Red plate.
Solid-fill the back red region with exact Beacon Red #C8102E to match the front's approved spot color.
Select the navy region of the back art for its Harbor Navy plate.
Solid-fill the back navy region with exact Harbor Navy #1B2A5B.
Select the cream/sand region of the back art to isolate its third plate.
Solid-fill the back cream region with exact Sand Cream #F0E6C8 so all three back colors are forced to their approved flat spot hexes.
Drop the paper-texture background on the back art to transparent so only the three-color artwork remains.
Isolate the Beacon Red areas on the cleaned back art for its separation plate.
Vectorize the back Beacon Red layer into a solid vector plate sized for ~30cm print width.
Isolate the Harbor Navy areas of the back art.
Vectorize the back Harbor Navy layer into a solid vector separation.

Select the two SATURATED brand colors together — 'all the bright-red and dark-navy artwork marks' — a robust high-contrast prompt, rather than trying to pick the near-white Sand Cream directly off a transparent background.

Invert the red+navy selection so the remaining opaque artwork pixels — the Sand Cream fill — become the third mask, deriving cream geometrically as NOT(red+navy) to avoid the shaky near-white-on-transparency selection.
Vectorize the back Sand Cream layer (the inverted-mask region) into the third back separation plate.
Vectorize the full cleaned three-color back art into one transparent-background composite SVG.

connector_workflow.inputs_from: front_sep_red_vector, front_sep_navy_vector, front_sep_cream_vector, front_composite.svg, back_sep_red_vector, back_sep_navy_vector, back_sep_cream_vector, back_composite.svg
connector_workflow.note: Initialize an upload session for all six separation plates plus the two composite SVGs into the staged project folders.
Finalize the upload; returns CC asset URNs for the plates/composites so they live in the tidy printer hand-off folder.

Render the two full-design composite vectors (front + back) into one two-page proof PDF — one placement per page — using document_render_vector's native per-artboard/document export. Each page carries all three approved spot colors in registration so the printer can eyeball the 3-color count and alignment; per-plate labelling and separation order live in the recipe note (n36), not baked into this render.

Emit the print-shop hand-off note: the three PANTONE numbers+hexes, front ~10cm / back ~30cm sizes, per-color separation order (mapping each uploaded separation plate URN to its PANTONE), mesh 156, and 'no underbase on light garment' guidance so the shop runs it without a call.

chaining_note: Strict output->input chaining across two parallel placement sub-pipelines that converge. FRONT: concept_front_chest.png seeds select_by_prompt(2)->fill_area(3)->select_by_prompt(4)->fill_area(5)->select_by_prompt(6)->fill_area(7)->single_color_saturation(8)->remove_background(9), each step consuming only the prior step's output; then the cleaned transparent art branches into three per-color separations — red select(10)->vectorize(11), navy select(12)->vectorize(13), and cream derived as NOT(red+navy): saturated-marks select(14)->invert_selection(15)->vectorize(16) — plus a full-art composite vectorize(17). BACK mirrors this exactly: concept_full_back.png -> select(18)->fill(19)->select(20)->fill(21)->select(22)->fill(23)->remove_background(24)-> [red select(25)->vectorize(26)], [navy select(27)->vectorize(28)], [cream: saturated-marks select(29)->invert_selection(30)->vectorize(31)], composite vectorize(32). All six separation vectors + two composite SVGs converge into the upload session(33)->finalize(34) (which also consumes project_folders from step 1), whose URNs stage the printer hand-off folder. The proof render(35) consumes only the two full-design composite vectors (front_composite.svg + back_composite.svg) and emits one two-page proof PDF (one placement per page) via document_render_vector's native per-document export — it does not montage all eight plates into one file. The recipe note(36) consumes the two supplied spec/palette references plus the uploaded URNs and carries the per-plate PANTONE labelling and separation order. Only step 1 (folder create) and the two raw concept loads originate outside a chain; every plate emerges from the assembled pipeline. 36 connector calls total.
difficulty_rationale: Long-horizon flagship: 36 connector calls, 10 distinct Adobe-server tools plus a local recipe emit — well above the >=10 calls / >=6 distinct-tool floor. Difficulty comes from running TWO separate raster concepts through a full spot-color separation pipeline: isolating each of three brand colors by prompt selection, FORCING each to its exact approved PANTONE hex as a SOLID flat fill (not a gradient or tint), desaturating and stripping a paper-texture background, then vectorizing every single-color layer into its own press-clean plate — six separation vectors plus two composites plus a two-page proof PDF. The near-white Sand Cream plate is derived geometrically as NOT(red+navy) — select the two saturated marks then image_invert_selection — rather than by an unreliable prompt-select of near-white on transparency. It respects every hard limit: image_fill_area is used only for SOLID flat fills (the entire point of spot-color seps), image_adjust_single_color_saturation only reduces the paper band's saturation (no recolor of brand art), image_invert_selection only flips an existing mask, image_remove_background only lifts to transparent, image_vectorize is genuine raster->vector, and no color outside the printer-approved 3-spot palette is introduced. document_render_vector exports the two composite vectors into a two-page proof (one placement per page) — no multi-file montage and no layout composed from scratch. No generative fill, no text-to-image/from-scratch art (the concepts and lettering are supplied flattened), no object-removal-with-reconstruction, no upscaling. Nothing is composed from scratch; the task strictly EDITS supplied artwork to a fixed spec.

reverify.banned_capability_check: None of the banned capabilities are used. image_fill_area (n3,n5,n7,n19,n21,n23) is SOLID flat spot-color fill only — forcing supplied regions to the approved PANTONE hexes — never generative fill/expand/reframe-crop. image_invert_selection (n15,n30) only flips an existing red+navy mask to yield cream (no new content). image_remove_background (n9,n24) only lifts artwork onto transparent; no object-removal-with-reconstruction/inpainting. image_vectorize traces supplied flat art. document_render_vector (n35) only EXPORTS the two connector-produced composite vectors into a two-page proof — no from-scratch layout, no montage-beyond-tool-scope. No text-to-image / from-scratch art or logo (concepts + lettering supplied flattened), no upscaling/super-resolution, no bespoke font design. All actions are real Adobe-connector operations; palette locked to the 3 supplied PANTONE spot colors. Gen-free confirmed.
reverify.notes: Both QA nits resolved. (b) Near-white Sand Cream is now derived geometrically (select saturated red+navy, then image_invert_selection -> cream = NOT(red+navy), then vectorize) on each placement, replacing the fragile near-white-on-transparency prompt-select; adds image_invert_selection as a 10th distinct Adobe tool. (a) document_render_vector now exports only the two composite vectors into a two-page proof (one placement per page) instead of montaging all 8 plates; per-plate PANTONE labels/order moved to the recipe note. Workflow renumbered to contiguous 1..36; tool_call_count 36, distinct_adobe_tools 10; all inputs_from resolve; tools_used, chaining_note, difficulty_rationale, outputs[proof].spec, and reverify reconciled. No IP/brand issues (fictional 'Little Lighthouse Fund'; PANTONE is an industry standard).
